metaData.Key Information:
- Kozhikoden's Special Murukku is a crispy and savory snack made from rice flour and spices
- It is a popular snack in Kerala, India, and is known for its unique flavor and texture
- The murukku is typically deep-fried or baked until golden brown, and it is often served with tea or coffee

metaData.Key Information: The papad is a thin Indian wafer, sometimes described as a cracker or flatbread - It is usually made from dried lentils eaten fried or roasted - It is served as an accompaniment to an Indian meal, as a snack and as croutons in soups - Papads are thin wafers made of urad dal

metaData.Key Information:
• Suvai Suvai Appalam/Keropok/Pappadum/Papad, a traditional delight that adds an irresistible crunch to your meals. Crafted with premium-quality lentil flour and aromatic spices, this ready-to-fry papad is a versatile side dish perfect for pairing with rice, curry, or even as a standalone snack.
• Whether you're hosting a dinner party or enjoying a family meal, Suvai Suvai Papadum elevates every dining experience with its rich flavor and crisp texture.
